title: blocked is a dead-end — no persisted state and no command to re-validate, advance the base, and write a corrected schema
description: A blocked outcome leaves the schema, block, and migrations.json byte-unchanged and records nothing actionable: resyncSchema's rollback restores migrations.json and returns {status:blocked, registeredMigrations:[]}, so the target schema version, the migration chain, and the per-item failure set are not persisted. Unlike a conflict — surfaced as {base,ours,theirs} and committed via resolve-conflict, which advances the merge base — blocked offers no resolution affordance. No command re-validates a corrected block against the target schema, advances the merge base, and writes the new schema.

evidence.reference: resyncSchema blocked path rolls everything back and persists no pending state
resolveConflict + stampBaselineFromBody — the conflict resolution template that commits a reconciliation and advances the merge base to the catalog so it converges
updateContext buckets a blocked schema by name only — no surfaced state, no affordance

impact: A blocked schema cannot be resolved through the tool: after fixing the items or local schema the operator re-runs full update, with no convergence affordance and no record of what to fix.
proposed_resolution: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
